Normal CI accepts reserved tests for planned/in-progress rows; implemented keep/adapt rows must resolve to one collected passing result. ## Redesign and drop rationale

<a id="export-dragging-piece-data-type"></a>

### `export.dragging-piece-data-type` — DraggingPieceDataType

**redesign.** A durable dragging-piece value lacks board identity, controlled base revision, input modality, and cancellation correlation required by the native intent lifecycle.

Native mapping: `MoveSource`, `MoveIntent`. Move payloads distinguish board and spare sources and carry revision/intent correlation.

Contract: `PARITY-EXPORT-DRAGGING-PIECE-DATA-TYPE`. Source: [src/types.ts:16-20](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/types.ts#L16-L20).

<a id="export-fen-string-to-position-object"></a>

### `export.fen-string-to-position-object` — fenStringToPositionObject

**redesign.** The upstream parser silently accepts malformed rows, treats multi-digit empties one digit at a time, and performs no dimension validation; native invalid input must fail atomically.

Native mapping: `parseFenPosition`. The native parser is strict, atomic, and permits FEN only for an 8x8 board.

Contract: `PARITY-EXPORT-FEN-STRING-TO-POSITION-OBJECT`. Source: [src/utils.ts:69-103](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/utils.ts#L69-L103).

<a id="export-get-position-updates"></a>

### `export.get-position-updates` — getPositionUpdates

**redesign.** The upstream utility embeds partial chess heuristics and chooses the first object-iteration candidate on ties, which can animate the wrong identical piece.

Native mapping: `inferPositionTransition`. Unique upstream movement heuristics remain only as a visual fallback; ambiguous ties fade or snap.

Contract: `PARITY-EXPORT-GET-POSITION-UPDATES`. Source: [src/utils.ts:123-274](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/utils.ts#L123-L274).

<a id="export-get-promotion-updates"></a>

### `export.get-promotion-updates` — getPromotionUpdates

**redesign.** Promotion and reversal inference is presentation-only and cannot decide game semantics or manufacture a canonical move.

Native mapping: `inferPromotionTransition`. Promotion inference is a safe fallback behind explicit transition hints and piece IDs.

Contract: `PARITY-EXPORT-GET-PROMOTION-UPDATES`. Source: [src/utils.ts:279-315](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/utils.ts#L279-L315).

<a id="export-is-touch-end-within-square"></a>

### `export.is-touch-end-within-square` — isTouchEndWithinSquare

**drop.** Native input uses one board-level gesture surface and measured coordinate hit testing; exposing a document-based helper would be unusable and misleading.

Native mapping: none for 1.0. DOM element lookup and React.TouchEvent hit testing have no public native equivalent.

Contract: `PARITY-EXPORT-IS-TOUCH-END-WITHIN-SQUARE`. Source: [src/utils.ts:422-449](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/utils.ts#L422-L449).

<a id="export-piece-drop-handler-args"></a>

### `export.piece-drop-handler-args` — PieceDropHandlerArgs

**redesign.** The upstream payload and synchronous Boolean callback cannot represent async validation, controlled commit correlation, cancellation, or spare-to-board routing.

Native mapping: `MoveIntent`. The move intent carries identity, source kind, target, revision, and input modality.

Contract: `PARITY-EXPORT-PIECE-DROP-HANDLER-ARGS`. Source: [src/types.ts:37-41](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/types.ts#L37-L41).

<a id="export-use-chessboard-context"></a>

### `export.use-chessboard-context` — useChessboardContext

**drop.** The upstream hook leaks internal shadow position, annotation, and browser-drag state; exporting it would make those implementation details a compatibility contract.

Native mapping: none for 1.0. The inferred 46-property browser context is intentionally private and has no public native hook.

Contract: `PARITY-EXPORT-USE-CHESSBOARD-CONTEXT`. Source: [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:134-134](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L134).

<a id="option-allow-auto-scroll"></a>

### `option.allow-auto-scroll` — allowAutoScroll

**drop.** Portable React Native code cannot generically control an arbitrary ancestor ScrollView while preserving deterministic destination geometry.

Native mapping: none for 1.0. No programmatic ancestor scrolling is promised for 1.0; gesture arbitration is still required.

Contract: `PARITY-OPTION-ALLOW-AUTO-SCROLL`. Source: [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:173-173](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L173), [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:249-249](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L249).

<a id="option-allow-drawing-arrows"></a>

### `option.allow-drawing-arrows` — allowDrawingArrows

**redesign.** The upstream gate controls an internal arrow list; native drawing must emit revisioned operations without accepting semantic state inside the component.

Native mapping: `ChessboardProps.annotationTool`, `ChessboardProps.onAnnotationOperation`. Drawing is enabled by a controlled native tool and operation handler.

Contract: `PARITY-OPTION-ALLOW-DRAWING-ARROWS`. Source: [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:177-177](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L177), [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:253-253](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L253).

<a id="option-arrows"></a>

### `option.arrows` — arrows

**redesign.** Combining external arrows with a persistent internal list creates dual ownership and cannot safely reconcile concurrent store updates.

Native mapping: `ChessboardProps.annotations`. Arrows become ID-bearing controlled annotations, with at most one transient draft.

Contract: `PARITY-OPTION-ARROWS`. Source: [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:178-178](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L178), [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:254-254](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L254).

<a id="option-clear-arrows-on-click"></a>

### `option.clear-arrows-on-click` — clearArrowsOnClick

**redesign.** The board cannot clear consumer-owned annotations itself; emitting a scoped operation avoids overwriting annotations added concurrently.

Native mapping: `ChessboardProps.annotationPolicies.clearOnBoardPress`, `ChessboardProps.onAnnotationOperation`. A policy emits a revisioned clear operation naming only observed annotation IDs.

Contract: `PARITY-OPTION-CLEAR-ARROWS-ON-CLICK`. Source: [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:180-180](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L180), [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:256-256](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L256).

<a id="option-clear-arrows-on-position-change"></a>

### `option.clear-arrows-on-position-change` — clearArrowsOnPositionChange

**redesign.** Upstream accidentally gates position-change clearing on clearArrowsOnClick and mutates internal state; native policy must be independent and store-driven.

Native mapping: `ChessboardProps.annotationPolicies.clearOnPositionChange`, `ChessboardProps.onAnnotationOperation`. The policy is independent of click clearing and emits a reasoned controlled operation.

Contract: `PARITY-OPTION-CLEAR-ARROWS-ON-POSITION-CHANGE`. Source: [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:181-181](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L181), [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:257-257](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L257).

<a id="option-on-arrows-change"></a>

### `option.on-arrows-change` — onArrowsChange

**redesign.** Emitting whole internal-arrow snapshots on effects can overwrite concurrent controlled updates and fires even for the initial empty state.

Native mapping: `ReactChessboardOptions.onArrowsChange`, `ChessboardProps.onAnnotationOperation`. The compatibility adapter reduces one operation against latest controlled arrows; the primary API emits the operation.

Contract: `PARITY-OPTION-ON-ARROWS-CHANGE`. Source: [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:185-185](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L185), [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:261-261](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L261).

<a id="option-on-mouse-out-square"></a>

### `option.on-mouse-out-square` — onMouseOutSquare

**drop.** Touch-first Android and iOS have no mouse-out event, and browser hover ordering is not a portable native contract.

Native mapping: none for 1.0. Pointer hover compatibility is deferred to post-1.0 React Native Web work.

Contract: `PARITY-OPTION-ON-MOUSE-OUT-SQUARE`. Source: [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:186-186](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L186), [src/ChessboardProvider.tsx:262-262](../../fixtures/parity/upstream-b74704a/src/ChessboardProvider.tsx#L262).

<a id="option-on-mouse-over-square"></a>

### `option.on-mouse-over-square` — onMouseOverSquare

**drop.** Touch-first Android and iOS have no mouse-over event, and browser hover ordering is not a portable native contract.

Native mapping: none for 1.0. Pointer hover compatibility is deferred to post-1.0 React Native Web work.

**redesign.** A synchronous Boolean result cannot correlate acceptance with a later controlled commit, cancel stale promises, or handle timeouts deterministically.

**redesign.** Upstream stores a mutable currentPosition mirror and mutates presentation snapshots; native rendering must always derive semantics from the latest controlled prop.

**redesign.** A Boolean browser animation switch cannot express the required system, always, and never reduced-motion policy or deterministic interruption semantics.

**redesign.** Suppressing animation through internal manual-drop flags treats a gesture result as accepted state before the consumer has committed it.

**drop.** React Native cannot generically discover and control arbitrary ancestor scrolling while retaining a stable measured destination.

**redesign.** Directly replacing a local position without cancelling asynchronous work allows stale gestures and timers to overwrite newer geometry.
